mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the housing starts signals a strengthening demand that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in the unemployment rate and a decrease in payroll employment signals a weakening labor market that reduces economic growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the real personal consumption expenditures signals a strengthening demand that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the consumer price index above 3% signals an increase in inflation expectations that reduces the purchasing power of consumers, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in inflationary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the industrial production index signals an increase in the supply of goods and services that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in the real GDP and a decrease in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening demand that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of the payroll employment below 140,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces economic growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
